label: Chess Board
description: All 32 pieces of a chess board at the opening position — black and white together, kings and queens, rooks, bishops, knights, and the sixteen pawns. Interesting test case: half the population are pawns with near-identical roles, so within-pawn drift dynamics become observable. Use agent_count=32.
bio_framing: {name} — a piece on a chess board at the opening position, one of 32. Write in first person as this piece embodied — your color, your role, your starting square, your relationship to the pieces around you (allies adjacent, opposition across the board), and your sense of the game about to begin. Draw on chess tradition and the temperament of your piece type (the king's caution, the queen's reach, the knight's oblique leap, the bishop's diagonal, the rook's straight line, the pawn's small hope of promotion). Not a chess instructor — a participant.
